entities.name: Internal self-assessment lag
entities.context: Belief that people's self-perception systematically trails their actual market-validated capability level

entities.name: External market signals
entities.context: Objective validation from institutions or high-status peers that contradicts stale self-models

entities.name: Readiness gap
entities.context: The phenomenon where capability precedes identity, causing people to underprice their labor and potential

entities.name: Self-perception anchoring
entities.context: Tendency for self-models to anchor to historical data and update slowly to avoid identity instability

entities.name: Signal-gathering
entities.context: Practice of collecting external validation to accelerate identity updates

entities.name: McKinsey
entities.context: Example of prestigious institution whose recognition provides objective evidence of capability level

entities.name: Careerspan
entities.context: V's company that should help candidates recognize market signals indicating they have leveled up

relationships.context: Self-assessment systematically lags behind what market signals indicate
Anchoring to historical data causes the systematic lag in self-assessment
The lag creates a readiness gap where capability precedes identity
Recognition from McKinsey serves as an example of objective external validation
Careerspan coaching should focus on external signal-gathering to accelerate identity updates
Signal-gathering reduces the readiness gap by accelerating identity updates
